items.title: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Microsoft SharePoint Vulnerability
items.description:
CISA says threat actors are exploiting a recently patched SharePoint remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2026-45659). 
 The post CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Microsoft SharePoint Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek .
items.apex_ai_summary: [CRITICAL] THREAT-INTEL advisory. Risk score 8.5/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items.ioc_note: Actor-attribution campaign — IOC set derived from threat actor research, not automated feed extraction.

apex.ai_summary: [P1] Vulnerability - critical severity (Risk 9.0/10). MITRE ATT&CK mapped: T1190 (1 technique). 1 indicator detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. AI confidence: LOW (25%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: CRITICAL PRIORITY - Patch within 24-48 hours. Activate IR playbook. Notify SOC lead and CISO.

ioc_paywall.message: 1 IOC(s) at 7.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.kev_name: Microsoft SharePoint Server Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability
items.kev_action: Apply mitigations in accordance with vendor instructions, ensuring compliance with CISA’s BOD 26-04 Prioritizing Security Updates Based on Risk (see URL in Notes) guidance and CISA’s “Forensics Triage Requirements” (see URL in Notes). Follow applicable BOD 26-04 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable. Stakeholders are responsible for evaluating each asset's internet exposure and ensuring adherence to BOD 26-04 patching guidelines.
items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Microsoft SharePoint Vulnerability
id: af80f9ee-7ac4-56c1-a35f-98668de8169f
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-45659 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: KEV_EXPLOIT
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-45659
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/02
modified: 2026/07/02
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-45659 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-02T21:19:45Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-45659", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-45659 Exploitation Attempt - KEV_EXPLOIT"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:7506554; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-02, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: CVE-2026-58138: Orkes Conductor 3.21.21 before 3.30.2 contains an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability that allows remote
items.description: Orkes Conductor 3.21.21 before 3.30.2 contains an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability that allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands by submitting inline workflow definitions containing malicious JavaScript or Python expressions to the workflow API endpoint prior to authentication. Attackers can exploit unsandboxed GraalVM evaluators configured with HostAccess.ALL or allowAllAccess(true) through INLINE, LAMBDA, DO_WHILE, and SWITCH task types to invoke arbitrary system commands via Java reflection or direct subprocess calls.

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-58138 Orkes Conductor 32121 before 3302 contains an unauthenticated rem
id: b95c21a0-07ba-53f3-8003-95a0a3fd5a06
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-58138 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-58138
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/02
modified: 2026/07/02
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-58138 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-02T21:19:45Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-58138", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-58138 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:4897316; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-02, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: High confidence based on: EPSS score 0.938 (very high exploitation probability); Source 'nvd_cve' is authoritative (government/standards body)
items.recommended_sla_action: PRIORITY PATCH — High severity with confirmed threat activity
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: CVE-2026-10109: IBM Db2 11.5.0 through 11.5.9, and 12.1.0 through 12.1.4 is vulnerable to remote code execution due to improper pre-auth

items.title: CVE-2025-71368: picklescan before 0.0.30 fails to detect the doctest.debug_script function when analyzing pickle files, allowing attacke
items.description: picklescan before 0.0.30 fails to detect the doctest.debug_script function when analyzing pickle files, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code. Remote attackers can craft malicious pickle files embedding doctest.debug_script calls that bypass picklescan detection and execute arbitrary commands upon pickle.load invocation.

items.title: CVE-2026-58449: txtai through 9.10.0, fixed in commit 11b32da, exposes an API /reindex endpoint whose function body parameter is resolve
items.description: txtai through 9.10.0, fixed in commit 11b32da, exposes an API /reindex endpoint whose function body parameter is resolved through txtai.util.Resolver, which performs __import__ and getattr on the caller-supplied dotted path with no allowlist. When the API is exposed with no TOKEN configured (authentication is opt-in, so all endpoints are unauthenticated) and the index is configured writable, a remote attacker can set function to an arbitrary callable such as subprocess.getoutput, achieving remote code execution as the server process during reindexing. Exploitation requires those deployment conditions (API exposed, no TOKEN, writable index); it is not the default configuration. The fix gates the endpoint behind a new reindex configuration flag.

items.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw Under Active Attack, 950 Systems Exposed
items.description: Oracle E-Business Suite flaw CVE-2026-46817 is under active attack, with about 950 vulnerable internet-facing instances still exposed. This week, Defused Cyber researchers warned that a critical vulnerability in Oracle E-Business Suite, tracked as CVE-2026-46817, is being actively exploited. The flaw affects Oracle Payments versions 12.2.3 through 12.2.15 and allows unauthenticated attackers to take over vulnerable […]

items.title: CVE-2025-71374: picklescan before 0.0.29 fails to detect the built-in python profile.Profile.run function when used in pickle reduce met
items.description: picklescan before 0.0.29 fails to detect the built-in python profile.Profile.run function when used in pickle reduce methods, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code. items.title: CVE-2025-71352: picklescan before 0.0.29 fails to detect the built-in Python trace.Trace.runctx function when used in pickle file reduce
items.description: picklescan before 0.0.29 fails to detect the built-in Python trace.Trace.runctx function when used in pickle file reduce methods, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code. Remote attackers can craft malicious pickle files with trace.Trace.runctx payloads that bypass picklescan detection and execute code upon pickle.load() invocation.

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2025-71352 picklescan before 0029 fails to detect the built-in Python traceT
id: d1a4cd52-5f76-5489-939e-aa5d896783dd
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2025-71352 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-71352
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/02
modified: 2026/07/02
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2025-71352 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-02T21:19:45Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2025-71352", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2025-71352 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:6051122; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-02, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: High confidence based on: EPSS score 0.637 (very high exploitation probability); Source 'nvd_cve' is authoritative (government/standards body)
items.recommended_sla_action: PRIORITY PATCH — High severity with confirmed threat activity
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: CVE-2025-71363: picklescan before 0.0.30 fails to detect cProfile.run function calls in pickle reduce methods, allowing attackers to exe
items.description: picklescan before 0.0.30 fails to detect cProfile.run function calls in pickle reduce methods, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code. Remote attackers can craft malicious pickle files with cProfile.run payloads that bypass picklescan detection and achieve code execution upon deserialization.

items.title: CVE-2025-71349: picklescan before 0.0.29 fails to detect the built-in trace.Trace.run function when analyzing pickle files, allowing att
items.description: picklescan before 0.0.29 fails to detect the built-in trace.Trace.run function when analyzing pickle files, allowing attackers to embed undetected malicious code. Remote attackers can craft malicious pickle files using trace.Trace.run in the reduce method to achieve arbitrary code execution when pickle.load processes the file.
